title: IRS Code 470 Meaning: Taxpayer Claim Pending | Tax Monitor Pro
description: IRS transcript code 470 meaning: Taxpayer Claim Pending. What it is, why it appears, common situations, and what to do next.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 470 Meaning

IRS Code 470 typically means the return has been processed and assessed. This page explains when it shows up on transcripts, what it usually signals, and practical next steps. Want to see what clients get? Open the sample report, then run your own transcript when you’re ready.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 470?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 470 means the return has been filed and assessed by the IRS, confirming the tax liability has been determined.

Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Usually appears near
Codes 806, 290, 570
Typical context
Processing, assessment, credit/payment activity
Client-friendly summary
"Your return was processed and assessed"

When does Code 470 appear?

  • 2–6 weeks after filing: Typically appears after e-filed returns are received and processed
  • With simple returns: May appear faster if return is straightforward
  • With complex returns: May take longer if additional review or schedules are needed

What does Code 470 usually signal?

If this appears alone

The return was assessed and no further action is visible yet. The refund or payment processing may be pending, or the transcript was pulled mid-cycle.

If this appears with Code 806 or 766/768

Code 470 confirmed the refund; Code 806 means it's being applied to next year's tax, while 766/768 means it's being issued as a check or direct deposit.

If this appears with Code 290 or 300

Code 470 confirmed a tax liability is due. The following code indicates a payment is owed and may be pending.

If this appears with Code 570

The refund was assessed but is temporarily held pending resolution of a discrepancy (W-2 match, ID verification, missing documentation, etc.).

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Confirm context: Review the date, adjacent codes, and amounts to understand the assessment timeline.
  2. Validate with client: Compare transcript codes to client records (returns filed, payments made, refunds expected).
  3. Communicate clearly: Provide a short written summary and next steps (refund timeline, payment due, hold reason, etc.).

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 470 appears in a client-ready transcript report with context:

Transcript Processing Timeline
Code 470 – Return filed and assessed
Date: 03/15/2024
Code 806 – Refund applied to next year
Amount: $2,450
Code 571 – Refund adjustment
Reason: Estimated tax payment reconciliation

FAQ

What is IRS Code 470?

IRS Code 470 indicates that the IRS has filed and assessed the return. It's a critical milestone that signals the return has been processed and the tax liability has been determined.

Does Code 470 mean my client's refund is approved?

Not necessarily. Code 470 means the return was assessed. If there's a refund due, it will be paired with Code 806 (applied to next year) or 766/768 (issued). If Code 290 or 300 appears, a payment is due instead.
